name: Night Visibility
summary: How much of the road you can actually read after dark.

name: Low Beam
summary: Headlight setting that reduces glare and preserves night vision.

name: Glare Control
summary: Managing bright lights so the road still stays readable.

name: Wet Surface
summary: Road moisture that changes grip, stopping, and steering.

name: Hydroplaning
summary: A loss of tire contact when water blocks the road surface.

name: Stopping Distance
summary: The full distance required to perceive, react, and stop.

name: Pedestrian Shadow
summary: A person that hides in the dark edge of the street or curb.

name: Bike Lane at Night
summary: A lane that must still be scanned for riders and surprise movement.

name: Fatigue Scan
summary: The disciplined scan used when tiredness starts to narrow attention.

name: Signal Timing
summary: Announcing your move early enough for others to react.

name: Bus Stop Caution
summary: Expecting crossings, luggage, and occlusion near stopping buses.

name: Reflection Reading
summary: Interpreting water and light on the road without assuming safety.
